architecture.summary: The paper proposes an LLM-as-a-Judge framework that uses large language models (specifically reasoning models like GPT-o3-mini) to automatically evaluate clinical EHR summaries generated by generative AI systems. The framework operates by having LLMs score summaries against the Provider Documentation Summarization Quality Instrument (PDSQI) rubric and comparing those automated scores to human evaluations. Information flows from generated clinical summaries into the LLM evaluator, which outputs quality scores that are then benchmarked against human reviewer scores.

biblio.title: Evaluating clinical AI summaries with large language models as judges

claims.statement: GPT-o3-mini achieved an intraclass correlation coefficient of 0.818 (95% CI 0.772-0.854)
GPT-o3-mini achieved a median score difference of 0 from humans

baseline.name: non-reasoning models, task-trained models, multi-agent approaches

claims.statement: Reasoning models excelled in inter-rater reliability, particularly for evaluations requiring advanced reasoning and domain expertise, outperforming non-reasoning, task-trained, and multi-agent approaches
LLM-as-a-Judge framework demonstrated strong inter-rater reliability with human evaluators when benchmarked against the validated Provider Documentation Summarization Quality Instrument (PDSQI)
